content.problemStatement:
There are NNN balls in a bag. Initially, the ithi^{th}ith ball has a number AiA_iAi​ on it.

In one second, a ball with number kkk (k>1)(k>1)(k>1), dissociates into kkk balls, where each of the kkk balls has number (k−1)(k-1)(k−1) written on it.

If k=1k = 1k=1, there is no change on the state of the ball.

Find the number of balls in the bag after 1010010^{100}10100 seconds. Since the final number of balls can be huge, output the answer modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting the number of balls in the bag.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the initial number written on the balls.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of balls in the bag after 1010010^{100}10100 seconds. Since the final number of balls can be huge, output the answer modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of balls. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the numbers written on the balls.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of balls in the bag after 10^100 seconds, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each ball we compute its factorial and sum them. Test1: 2! = 2. Test2: 1! + 2! = 1 + 2 = 3. Test3: 3! + 1! + 3! = 6 + 1 + 6 = 13.

solution.approach: Each ball evolves independently. A ball with initial number k splits into k balls with number k‑1 after one second, each of those splits into (k‑1) balls with number k‑2, and so on until the number becomes 1. The total number of final balls produced by this single ball is k·(k‑1)·…·1 = k! (k factorial). Therefore the final total number of balls is the sum of factorials of all initial numbers: answer = Σ (Ai!). We pre‑compute factorials modulo MOD for all values up to the maximum Ai appearing in the whole input (≤10^6) in O(maxAi) time, then answer each test case in O(N) by summing the pre‑computed values.
solution.timeComplexity: O(maxAi + total N) where maxAi ≤ 10^6 and total N ≤ 2·10^5.

code.Python3:
import sys
MOD = 1_000_000_007

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    test_cases = []
    max_a = 0
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        test_cases.append(arr)
        max_a = max(max_a, max(arr))
    # precompute factorials
    fact = [1] * (max_a + 1)
    for i in range(1, max_a + 1):
        fact[i] = fact[i-1] * i % MOD
    out_lines = []
    for arr in test_cases:
        total = sum(fact[x] for x in arr) % MOD
        out_lines.append(str(total))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
